text:
Nanoparticle (NP) synthesis is rapidly moving towards green nanotechnology due to the escalating need for economical and eco-friendly process alternatives to the conventional expensive physical-chemical methods. Moreover, the metal oxide nanoparticles have gained substantial importance due to their remarkable thermal, optical and electronic properties. In this study, eco-friendly synthesis of zinc oxide nanoparticles (ZnO-NPs) using aqueous date palm extract (DPE) rich in polyphenols as a green reducing agent is presented.

In order to make the synthesis process efficient, the effect of different concentrations of precursor solution and reducing agent on the prepared nanostructures was investigated. The prepared NPs were characterized by XRD, TEM, SEM and FTIR. The results show that crystalline ZnO-NPs with varying particle sizes in the range of 18.1-61.6 nm were prepared. In addition to structural studies, the obtained bio-fabricated NPs were evaluated for their antioxidant activity using total antioxidant capacity (TAC) assay and free radical scavenging activity using DPPH assay and antibacterial studies against the human pathogens Staphylococcus aureus (Gram positive) and Escherichia coli (Gram negative). The results suggest that plant-mediated synthesis can be an efficient and non-toxic method for producing active metallic nanoparticles compared with the conventional toxic chemical-based methods.

However, the presence of specific experimental details (sizes, methods) could reflect real work, so the assessment is uncertain.
